title: Partial Staging with Patch Mode
description: You've made multiple unrelated edits to the same file `server.js` — a bug fix in the `handleRequest` function and a WIP experimental feature at the bottom. Both edits are already present in `server.js` in the working tree. You need to commit only the bug fix as one clean commit, leaving the experimental code uncommitted.
objective: Exactly one new commit must exist that modifies `server.js` and contains only the bug fix (the `handleRequest` function change). The experimental code must be present in the working tree but NOT staged and NOT committed. The working tree must show `server.js` as modified (unstaged).
hints: `git add -p server.js` enters patch mode and lets you approve or skip individual hunks.
Answer `y` to stage a hunk, `n` to skip it, `s` to split a hunk into smaller pieces.
After staging only the bug fix hunk, `git commit` commits just that portion.
